summary: Chris Lee held a sprint retrospective with Tyler Washington and Ravi Gupta to review the reliability sprint initiated after a major March outage. The team celebrated key achievements including redundant processing nodes and a circuit breaker implementation, with failover times hitting 12-14 seconds against a 30-second target. The team identified monitoring gaps in the event ingestion pipeline and discussed plans for a pipeline health dashboard. Chris proposed formalizing quarterly reliability-focused sprints and organizing a knowledge share with the broader engineering organization.
actionItems: Tyler Washington: Write up the pipeline health dashboard as a formal Jira ticket before EOD
Chris Lee: Set up a knowledge share session with the broader engineering group in a couple weeks once the runbook is polished
Tyler Washington: Lead a live walkthrough of the circuit breaker implementation for the engineering knowledge share
Chris Lee: Loop in leadership to officially establish quarterly reliability-focused sprints

keyMoments.text: Chris praises the team for shipping the redundant processing nodes and circuit breaker implementation cleanly following the March outage
keyMoments.speaker: Chris Lee

keyMoments.text: Ravi reveals failover times are hitting 12-14 seconds in testing, significantly beating the 30-second target
keyMoments.speaker: Ravi Gupta

keyMoments.text: Ravi flags that the event ingestion pipeline lacked proper health alerting, which contributed to the severity of the March outage
keyMoments.speaker: Ravi Gupta

keyMoments.text: Chris commits to formalizing quarterly reliability-focused sprints and looping in leadership to make it official
keyMoments.speaker: Chris Lee
